text.title: Kubernetes Cost Optimisation Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules grounding the finops-devops-cost-kubernetes methodology, each paired with rationale and source reference.

rule.statement: Split node pools by workload class — system, on-demand stateful, Spot batch, GPU — and taint Spot + GPU pools so only tolerating workloads land on them.
rule.rationale: Mixing classes blocks autoscaler from down-scaling and forces over-provisioning for the worst-case pod.

rule.statement: Set CPU requests to observed P95 and memory requests + limits equal; do not set CPU limits unless throttling is required.
rule.rationale: Memory limit ≠ request triggers OOMKill on bursts; CPU limits at request level cause throttling under load.

rule.statement: Use HPA on a business-level metric (RPS, queue depth) or P95 CPU, with min replicas ≥ 2 for production workloads and stabilizationWindowSeconds set on scale-down.
rule.rationale: CPU-only HPA lags traffic spikes; aggressive scale-down without a stabilisation window thrashes the cluster.

rule.statement: Apply ResourceQuota + LimitRange in every tenant namespace and enforce required cost-allocation labels at admission.
rule.rationale: Without quotas one team's runaway job can starve the whole cluster; labels keep chargeback honest.

rule.statement: Track utilisation per workload via kubecost or OpenCost and alert when cluster CPU utilisation falls below 35% or memory below 50% sustained over 7 days.
rule.rationale: Sub-threshold utilisation is the kubernetes-specific signal that node-pool sizing has drifted from reality.

rule.statement: If preconditions in `Applies If` are not all true, do NOT run this methodology — return early with reason.
rule.rationale: Decision tree's negative branch must terminate cleanly without partial output.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold AND scope is explicit, run the methodology end-to-end and produce the canonical artefact.
rule.rationale: Decision tree positive-branch terminus; aliases the apply-end-to-end verdict.
